command.mythos.taildebug_given: Tail tuner granted. J/L = X, I/K = Y, U/O = Z, 7/9 = RX, 4/6 = RY, 1/3 = RZ, [/] = step, P = reset, H = show.
message.mythos.choose_prompt: Choose a myth. Use /mythos choose to open the selection screen again.
message.mythos.smithing_locked: You cannot forge this item with your current myth.
message.mythos.elf_only_forge: Only elves can forge the Elven Bow.
message.mythos.dwarf_only_tempering: Only dwarves can temper an enchanted pickaxe with a netherite ingot.
myth.mythos.human.description: A flexible myth of towns and villages that shines brightest through trade and reputation.
myth.mythos.human.advantage.extended_tra: Temporarily gains twice the trade stock while dealing with villagers.
myth.mythos.human.advantage.trade_insigh: Sees the remaining stock of the selected trade.
myth.mythos.human.disadvantage.village_d: Its strongest advantages only matter around villages and villagers.
myth.mythos.human.guide.feature.full_hei: Humans keep the standard height of about 2 blocks, with no racial model changes.
myth.mythos.human.guide.feature.heroic_r: Humans constantly maintain Hero of the Village and also press most deals close to their minimum price, making village trade especially efficient.
myth.mythos.human.guide.feature.extended: When a human trades with a villager, each offer temporarily gets double stock, letting that villager yield far more books, goods, and emerald loops before locking.
myth.mythos.human.guide.feature.trade_in: Inside the trade screen, a human can see how many uses remain on the selected offer. This makes it easier to squeeze the exact trade you need before it locks.
myth.mythos.elf.description: A forest myth built around grace, keen archery, and access to elven forging.
myth.mythos.elf.advantage.forest_stride: Gain speed in forest biomes and cherry groves.
myth.mythos.elf.advantage.archery_master: Deal increased damage with bows and arrows.
myth.mythos.elf.advantage.elven_bow_forg: Can forge the Elven Bow with improved drops.
myth.mythos.elf.guide.feature.full_heigh: Elves keep the standard height of about 2 blocks, without changing the player model.
myth.mythos.elf.guide.feature.forest_str: Speed applies in forest, flower forest, birch forest, old growth birch forest, dark forest, taiga, old growth pine taiga, old growth spruce taiga, and cherry grove.
myth.mythos.elf.guide.feature.archery_ma: Bows and arrows hit harder for elves, while axes are noticeably weaker.
myth.mythos.elf.guide.feature.ale_reacti: Elves can drink dwarven ale, but it leaves them nauseous.
myth.mythos.elf.guide.crafting.elven_bow: At a smithing table, an elf can temper a bow with a netherite ingot to forge an Elven Bow with stronger drops.
myth.mythos.dwarf.description: A stone-bred master of the mines who stands shorter, digs faster, and works best with a beer close at hand.
myth.mythos.dwarf.advantage.deep_mining: Below Y=0, mining becomes even faster.
myth.mythos.dwarf.advantage.runic_temper: Can temper pickaxe enchantments in a smithing table with a netherite ingot.
myth.mythos.dwarf.disadvantage.ale_depen: Without ale, gains Slowness I, and after a long drought also suffers Blindness.
myth.mythos.dwarf.guide.feature.compact_: Dwarves stand lower than normal players and settle around a height of roughly 1.5 blocks.
myth.mythos.dwarf.guide.feature.stone_st: Dwarves always mine faster, and the bonus grows deeper than Y=0.
myth.mythos.dwarf.guide.feature.runic_te: An enchanted pickaxe can be tempered with a netherite ingot to raise ordinary enchantment levels by 1, but only once.
myth.mythos.dwarf.guide.feature.ale_depe: Without the Ale Spirit effect, a dwarf gains Slowness I, and after thirty minutes without ale, Blindness starts stacking on top.
myth.mythos.dwarf.guide.crafting.dwarven: Dwarven ale is brewed from a honey bottle and two wheat, and the empty bottle is returned after drinking.
myth.mythos.dwarf.guide.crafting.tempere: Pickaxe tempering only works for dwarves: place an enchanted pickaxe and a netherite ingot into the smithing table.
myth.mythos.fairy.description: A tiny airy myth that flits close to the ground, folds under pressure, and lives by the sky.
myth.mythos.fairy.advantage.low_flight: Can freely fly while there are 5 blocks or less to solid ground below.
myth.mythos.fairy.advantage.wind_artifac: Can forge Fairy Boots and a Fairy Minecart from wind charges.
myth.mythos.fairy.advantage.shared_visio: Can actively grant night vision to nearby players.
myth.mythos.fairy.disadvantage.fragile_b: Maximum health is cut in half.
myth.mythos.fairy.disadvantage.melee_wea: Melee weapon damage is reduced by 30%.
myth.mythos.fairy.guide.feature.tiny_fra: Fairies shrink to about 1 block tall, making them visibly smaller than most myths.
myth.mythos.fairy.guide.feature.low_flig: If there are no more than 5 blocks down to the nearest solid block, a fairy can fly freely almost like in creative mode. Once it has switched into elytra mode, free flight does not come back on its own: near support, the fairy must press Shift.
myth.mythos.fairy.guide.feature.wind_art: With a wind charge, fairies can forge two artifacts in the smithing table: Fairy Boots to speed up running and a Fairy Minecart that removes the usual speed cap from powered rails.
myth.mythos.fairy.guide.feature.shared_v: By pressing %s, a fairy grants Night Vision to all players within 5 blocks for a short time. The ability then goes on cooldown.
myth.mythos.fairy.guide.crafting.fairy_b: Fairy Boots can only be forged by fairies: place any boots and a wind charge into the smithing table.
myth.mythos.fairy.guide.crafting.fairy_m: The Fairy Minecart can only be forged by fairies: place a normal minecart and a wind charge into the smithing table.
myth.mythos.kitsune.description: A fox myth of trickery and night hunting: it dons a mask after dusk, slips away with a dash, hurls foxfire, and forges mystical artifacts.
myth.mythos.kitsune.advantage.night_mask: At night it can wear a mask, gain night vision, and call foxfire.
myth.mythos.kitsune.advantage.fox_dash: Can dash quickly and throw foxfire.
myth.mythos.kitsune.advantage.fox_artifa: Can forge a Fox Lantern and Fox Whistle for other players.
myth.mythos.kitsune.disadvantage.day_mas: During the day, the mask and tails become visual only and grant no night powers.
myth.mythos.kitsune.disadvantage.melee_w: Melee weapon damage is reduced by 30%.
myth.mythos.kitsune.guide.feature.full_h: The kitsune keeps the standard 2-block height. It has clear fox ears and a large tail, and at night small foxfires begin to orbit around the mask.
myth.mythos.kitsune.guide.feature.night_: Press %s to show or hide the kitsune mask and tails together. During the day this is purely visual, while at night or in dimensions without daylight the mask also grants Night Vision and invisibility while sneaking.
myth.mythos.kitsune.guide.feature.foxfir: Pressing %s launches foxfire only while the mask is active and its night powers are actually awake. In daylight or under bright sky, the mask remains visual only.
myth.mythos.kitsune.guide.feature.fox_da: Press %s to dash sharply in the direction you are looking, including upward. This works during the day as well, so the myth keeps its mobility before sunset.
myth.mythos.kitsune.guide.feature.heaven: While masked, the first successful melee strike calls down Heavenly Wrath: lightning hits the target. After triggering, the effect goes on a short cooldown.
myth.mythos.kitsune.guide.feature.fox_ar: Kitsune can forge two artifacts for other players. A placed Fox Lantern lures nearby hostile mobs toward itself, while a Fox Whistle calls those mobs onto its user instead.
myth.mythos.kitsune.guide.crafting.fox_l: The Fox Lantern can only be forged by kitsune: place a lantern and a glow ink sac into the smithing table. Once placed, it starts pulling hostile mobs toward itself.
myth.mythos.kitsune.guide.crafting.fox_w: The Fox Whistle can only be forged by kitsune: place a goat horn and a fire charge into the smithing table.
myth.mythos.siren.description: A myth of the deep sea: breathes underwater forever, swims swiftly, and must keep close to water even while walking on land.
myth.mythos.siren.advantage.water_breath: Always breathes underwater and sees clearly in the deep.
myth.mythos.siren.advantage.swift_swim: Swims much faster than a normal player.
myth.mythos.siren.advantage.siren_elixir: Can prepare a Siren Elixir from a water bottle and nautilus shell.
myth.mythos.siren.disadvantage.dry_land: Starts suffocating on dry land if it stays away from water too long.
myth.mythos.siren.guide.feature.full_hei: The siren keeps a standard player height of about 2 blocks, without changing the base model.
myth.mythos.siren.guide.feature.water_br: Underwater, the siren always breathes, sees clearly in the deep, and does not lose air like a normal player.
myth.mythos.siren.guide.feature.swift_sw: In water, the siren gains a strong swimming boost from permanent dolphin-like grace, making oceans, rivers, and underwater ruins its natural territory.
myth.mythos.siren.guide.feature.dry_land: On land, the siren uses an inverted air meter: its bubbles tick down while walking in the dry. Once the meter empties, it starts taking drying damage. A regular water bottle instantly refills the air bar. Marine enchantments also flip with it: Respiration on the helmet slows drying, Depth Strider on boots softens the land slowness and removes it completely at level III, and Aqua Affinity on the helmet removes the dry-land mining penalty.
myth.mythos.siren.guide.crafting.siren_e: The Siren Elixir can only be forged by a siren: place a water bottle and a nautilus shell into the smithing table. The elixir grants water breathing, swift swimming, and underwater vision for thirty minutes.
tooltip.mythos.elven_bow: Elven forging empowers drops from creatures slain with it.
tooltip.mythos.dwarven_pickaxe: Tempered with dwarven rune-work to raise ordinary enchantments by one level.
tooltip.mythos.dwarven_ale: Keeps a dwarf working for a full hour.
tooltip.mythos.fairy_boots: Fairy winds make every step lighter and quicker.
tooltip.mythos.fairy_minecart: This minecart does not obey the usual speed cap on powered rails.
tooltip.mythos.fox_lantern: Place the lantern to lure nearby hostile mobs toward it.
tooltip.mythos.fox_whistle: On use, it draws nearby hostile mobs onto the wielder.
tooltip.mythos.siren_elixir: Grants water breathing, swift swimming, and deep vision for thirty minutes.
tooltip.mythos.kitsune_tail_tuner.line1: Hold it to move the kitsune tail.
tooltip.mythos.kitsune_tail_tuner.line2: J/L = X, I/K = Y, U/O = Z, 7/9 = RX.
tooltip.mythos.kitsune_tail_tuner.line3: 4/6 = RY, 1/3 = RZ, [/] = step, P = reset, H = show.
tooltip.mythos.mythos_guide: Opens a guide to myths, their traits, and their unique crafting.
message.mythos.fairy_only_artifacts: Only a fairy can forge these artifacts.
message.mythos.fairy_vision_cooldown: Fairy vision will recover in %s sec.
message.mythos.kitsune_mask_night_only: Kitsune can wear the mask only at night.
message.mythos.kitsune_mask_day_visual: During the day, the mask and tails are visual only, without night powers.
message.mythos.kitsune_dash_cooldown: Fox dash will recover in %s sec.
message.mythos.kitsune_only_artifacts: Only a kitsune can forge these fox artifacts.
message.mythos.siren_only_elixir: Only a siren can prepare the Siren Elixir.
message.mythos.siren_drying_out: The siren is drying out. Touch water or drink a water bottle quickly.
message.mythos.siren_water_restored: A water bottle restored your moisture.
gui.mythos.selection.title: Mythos
gui.mythos.guide.title: Tome of Mythos
gui.mythos.no_myths: There are no myths available yet.
